title: A protocol of genome-wide association study in Han Chinese for primary Sjögren’s syndrome
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"pjdp1","text":"Primary Sjögren’s syndrome is one of the most common autoimmune diseases. To identify novel genetic susceptibility loci for primary Sjögren’s syndrome, we performed a three-stage genome-wide association study (GWAS) in Han Chinese. At the discovery stage, 556,134 autosomal SNPs were analyzed in 542 cases and 1,050 controls. Promising associations were validated in two-stage replications with 1,303 cases and 2,727 controls. The combined analysis identified a new susceptibility locus for primary Sjögren’s syndrome. Our analysis also confirmed previously reported primary Sjögren’s syndrome associations in Europeans in several loci. Fine mapping of the region around the new locus confirmed the association and further extended the region of the new locus.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: This document outlines the protocols for conducting a genome-wide association study (GWAS) focusing on identifying single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs).
Prepare DNA samples by ensuring they are at the appropriate concentration and purity for genotyping.
Select genotyping array compatible with your study's design.
Load the DNA samples into the genotyping array according to the manufacturer's instructions.
Run the genotyping assay to generate SNP data.
Import SNP data into your chosen analysis software.
Conduct quality control checks to remove any poorly performing markers or samples.
Perform statistical tests to identify associations between SNPs and the trait of interest.
Validate the top SNPs in an independent dataset if available.
Summarize the key findings and consider potential biological relevance.
The authors declare no competing financial interests.

A genome-wide association study in Han Chinese identifies a susceptibility locus for primary Sjögren's syndrome at 7q11.23 by Yongzhe Li, Kunlin Zhang, Hua Chen, Fei Sun, Juanjuan Xu, Ziyan Wu, Ping Li, Liuyan Zhang, Yang Du, Haixia Luan, Xi Li, Lijun Wu, Hongbin Li, Huaxiang Wu, Xiangpei Li, Xiaomei Li, Xiao Zhang, Lu Gong, Lie Dai, Lingyun Sun, Xiaoxia Zuo, Jianhua Xu, Huiping published in Nature Genetics on 09 Sep, 2013 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ng.2779
creator: Protocols Importer
